title: Closed: Home Child Care Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot: Gaining experience category - Canada.ca
content:
## Closed: Home Child Care Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot: Gaining experience category
We’re no longer accepting new applications
The pilots ended on June 17, 2024. We’ll continue to process applications we received on or before this date.
Learn more about the new Home Care Worker Immigration pilots and how to apply.
The Home Child Care Provider Pilot and the Home Support Worker Pilot were 5-year pilot programs that let qualified caregivers and their family members come to Canada with the goal of becoming permanent residents.

## Gaining experience category
The Gaining experience category was for applicants who never worked full-time as a caregiver or didn’t have enough work experience to qualify directly for permanent residence. Here’s how it worked:
- You applied for a work permit application with your permanent residence application.
- If you met the requirements, we gave you a work permit to work as a caregiver.
- Once you had enough work experience, you could send us proof and we would make a final decision on your permanent residence application.

### Work experience you need for all applications in progress
You need 6 months of eligible work experience from within or outside Canada to qualify for permanent residence.
If you already applied and now have enough experience, send us proof .

### Work experience you need if we already made a decision on your application
If we sent you a decision on your application before June 16, 2024, there were different requirements .
The deadline to apply was June 17, 2024
